title: AtomDefinition
description: Schema for `config/stage-atoms/<id>.yaml`. Plan §S4.3 — validates AtomDefinition shape before deserialization (`include_str!` schema-validate-before-deserialize discipline). Mirrors the `crates/core/src/atom.rs::AtomDefinition` Rust type. Per plan §S4.3: role==discovery requires discovery_kind, no `..` in paths, assignee enum, EDAM IRI regex constrains the operation/data/format namespaces.

id.description: Stable atom id. Lowercase + underscores; convention is verb_noun.

version.description: Semver. Phase 1 records the field; Phase 3 enforces uniqueness across the registry.

role.description: Drives builder-side expansion semantics. Plan §A.S1 — `selection` lifts the legacy `select_*` filename prefix into a typed role; `calibration`/`pilot`/`adversarial`/`monitor` are speculative variants that default to `operation`-equivalent behavior at consumer sites until specialized.

discovery_kind.description: Required when role == discovery. Names the kind of decision the agent makes (method, threshold, panel, etc.).

description.description: Free-form English description; surfaces in CONTEXT.md + agent prompts.

edam_operation.description: EDAM operation IRI or `swfc:` extension per ADR 0004.

edam_data.description: EDAM data class IRI for primary input. **DEPRECATED in Phase 16 of the DAG composer alignment plan** — superseded by the typed `inputs:` array. Retained for migration / synthesis fallback only; new atoms should declare `inputs:` and `outputs:` directly. The synthesis fallback in `crates/core/src/workflow_contracts/from_atom.rs::synthesize_inputs` still consumes this field when `inputs:` is absent.

edam_format.description: EDAM format IRI for primary committed artifact. **DEPRECATED in Phase 16 of the DAG composer alignment plan** — superseded by `outputs:` array entries' `physical_format` field. See `edam_data` deprecation note.

assignee.description: Whoever runs this atom. Closed enum.

depends_on.description: Atom ids this atom requires output from.

excludes.description: Mutually-exclusive atoms. Atom-id form (lowercase snake_case) maps to a literal id reference; `cel:<expression>` form (Phase 3 / S7.3) compiles the CEL at registry load (`AtomRegistry::validate_consistency`) and the composer evaluates it against the (intake, <task_id>.result, self) context at compose time.

attributes.description: Free-form attribute map; the discover_* runtime selector reads compatible subsets.

joint_with.description: Plan §S7.10 — multi-modal joint-source constraints. Each entry pairs two upstream atom ids that must share `attributes.source_atom`. Used by integration atoms (e.g. multi-modal scRNA-seq + CITE-seq).

inputs.description: Phase 2 of the DAG composer alignment plan — explicit rich PortContract input ports. Optional and additive: when absent, the compatibility::engine synthesizes coarse ports from edam_data / edam_format. Each entry mirrors crates/core/src/workflow_contracts/port.rs::PortContract.

outputs.description: Phase 2 of the DAG composer alignment plan — explicit rich PortContract output ports. See `inputs` for the migration story.

deferred_to.description: Atom id of the discovery step that produces the choice.

image.description: OCI image reference. No `..` segments allowed (defense in depth against path-style attacks).

digest.description: SHA-256 digest. Empty in atom YAML; emitter populates per ADR 0025.

network.description: Plan §S15.1/§S15.9 — typed NetworkPolicy. `{kind: bridge}` (default) or `{kind: none, allowlist: [...]}`. Pre-S15.1 string shorthand ("bridge"|"none") is rejected; rewrite atom YAMLs.

source.description: Plan §S15.1/§S15.13 — typed ContainerSource. Default `{kind: image}`. `{kind: conda, conda_packages}` routes through Wave/Seqera (S15.13); `{kind: host}` opts back to bare-host execution.

claim_boundary.description: claim_boundary directive carried into emitted package's interpretation policy.

condition.description: Plan §S7.3 follow-on — atom-level CEL gate. Threaded onto StageSpec::condition by the composer adapter; builder's propagate_readiness evaluates against upstream results at runtime. Returning false short-circuits the task to TaskState::Skipped. Example: 'discover_batch_correction.result.batch_correction_required == true'.

required_figures.description: Figure ids the atom must produce under runtime/outputs/<stage_id>/figures/. Composer threads this to Task.spec.required_figures.

figure_exempt.description: Phase 4 figure-obligation lint — marks this atom as explicitly exempt from the obligation that every DataProduct output resolve to a non-Deferred PlotAffordance. Required when an atom produces data that has no registered affordance and either (a) is a benchmark/eval atom, (b) is a discovery-only atom, or (c) is pending Phase 10 affordance migration.

plot_stage_id.description: Optional shared plotting module id when the emitted stage id is an alias or spelling variant.

required_artifacts.description: Artifacts the harness must verify before accepting task completion. Paths are relative to runtime/outputs/<stage_id>/.

validators.description: Phase 13/16 — Validation-obligation ids the harness runs against this atom's emitted artifacts after task completion. Each id resolves against the runner registry (ValidatorRegistry); typical values: p_value_in_unit_interval, gene_id_in_annotation, coordinate_in_contig, barcode_matrix_dim_consistency, no_train_test_leakage, deterministic_or_bounded_variance. Threaded through to RequiredArtifact.validation_obligations by both the v4 lowering pass and the legacy build_dag_from_composition path. Empty default — atoms without obligations pass through unchanged.

runtime_packages.description: Plan §S15.x derived-image warm-up — system + language packages this atom requires at runtime. The emitter aggregates the union of every reachable atom's declarations into policies/runtime-prereqs.json. At the atom level only system_packages, language_packages, and system_check are meaningful — base_image and modality belong on the archetype/taxonomy and are ignored here.

iterate.description: Plan §S10.1 — iterate-until block. Required when the atom's compiled stage carries Cardinality::IterateUntil; the builder emits a 4-template-task scaffold (iterate_gate / placeholder / iterate_check / validate) and the agent fans out a linear chain of <id>_iter_N tasks until convergence.

max_iterations.description: Hard ceiling. Reached without convergence → BlockerKind::IterationDidNotConverge.

min_iterations.description: Lower bound; default 1. Schema rejects min > max via the at-load-time validator (the JSON Schema can't compare two siblings, so the cross-check lives in atom_registry::validate_consistency).

metric_source.description: CEL expression naming the metric field on result.json (e.g. `result.silhouette`, `result.gradient_norm`). Compiled by the agent at iteration time, not by the composer.

operator.description: Comparison operator. lt for loss-decreasing, gt for silhouette-rising, etc.

threshold.description: Numeric threshold the metric is compared against.

consecutive_iterations.description: How many consecutive iterations must satisfy the rule before declaring convergence. Default 2 — one stable pass rules out a stochastic dip.

on_max_iterations.description: What the harness does when max_iterations hits without convergence. Default block surfaces BlockerKind::IterationDidNotConverge so the SME picks (raise / accept / abort).

best_selector.description: Optional CEL field-path the agent uses to pick the 'best' iteration when on_max_iterations == accept_best. Without it, the last iteration is the default.

allOf.comment: Plan §S4.3 — no `..` segments in any string-valued field. Defense-in-depth against atom yaml that tries to escape its directory via `description` or `image`. JSON Schema can't do recursive negative pattern, so this is enforced on the path-bearing fields above (image) and at load time by the registry.
